schoolName: Hof University of Applied Sciences (Hochschule Hof) - Cross Cultural Nursing Practice M.Sc.
reviewNotes: Corroborated across Hof University's own official recruitment flyer for the programme (Flyer_CCN_web_01-2025.pdf, hosted on hof-university.com), the official module/ECTS breakdown PDF (Studienverlaufsplan, Faculty INWISS), Hof University's own news article and the independent trade press (Bibliomed Pflege / campuls, the university's news portal) covering the programme's April 2023 launch announcement, and independent general-reference sources (English Wikipedia for Hof University and for the town of Hof) for university/city background facts. IMPORTANT STATUS CAVEAT, flagged prominently in the content: multiple independent search results (Hof University's own current programme-search context, mastersportal/master-and-more aggregators, and Studis Online) indicate the master's programme - originally named 'Cross Cultural Nursing Practice' (M.Sc./M.A., launched 2024) - is currently being revised and is expected to relaunch under a new name, 'Advanced Nursing Practice (M.Sc.)', for winter semester 2026/27, with full updated admission requirements to be published by Hof University closer to the April 2026 application window. This draft is built from the most recent official materials available (the 2025 flyer and official module plan for 'Cross Cultural Nursing Practice'), which describe the programme design, structure, fees and requirements that formed the basis of the current offering; because the exact final name/requirements for the 2026/27 relaunch were not independently confirmed at time of writing, the content explicitly tells students to verify the current programme name and requirements directly with Hof University before applying, rather than asserting the relaunch details as settled fact. Language of instruction is reported precisely as the official flyer states it (English in semester 1, German from semester 2 onward) - this is a genuine mixed-medium programme, not fully English-taught, and is flagged as a things-to-consider item. Indian Nursing Council (INC) recognition for future practice in India is explicitly NOT asserted; students are told to verify INC equivalency themselves, since INC assesses foreign nursing qualifications case-by-case rather than blanket-recognising any single foreign programme. No tuition amount is fabricated: the programme is confirmed tuition-free by the official flyer (only a ~EUR150/semester administrative fee), so no annualTuitionUsd/totalTuitionUsd figures are included; only the confirmed no-tuition-fee status and the administrative fee are stated narratively with a source. No official on-campus hostel fee schedule was found, so hostel figures are omitted and students are told to confirm accommodation/support routes (the flyer only lists 'assistance in finding accommodation' as a service, not a fee schedule). No single-source claims were used for anything material - every substantive fact (programme design, fees, city, university founding year) has at least one independent corroborating source beyond Hof University's own materials.

sources.label: Hof University - Cross Cultural Nursing Practice (M.Sc.) official recruitment flyer (PDF)
sources.notes: Official Hof University flyer (hosted on hof-university.com) confirming: Master of Science degree, 4 semesters including the Master's thesis; language of instruction English in semester 1, German from semester 2 onward (internships and thesis primarily in German, thesis optionally in English); no tuition fee, only an administrative fee of approx. EUR 150/semester; academic entry requirement of a completed Bachelor's degree in Nursing (min. 180 ECTS); English proficiency (TOEFL 90+ or IELTS 6.5+) plus German at minimum A2 (CEFR) at entry, built up to B2 during the programme; core modules (Intercultural Trainings, Culture-Sensitive Care, Sector-Specific Assessments/Diagnostics/Intervention, Evidence Based Nursing Research Workshop, Healing Competences Basics/I/II, Clinical Leadership); two clinical internships (semester 3) at a hospital, long-term inpatient or home-care institution, totalling at least 900 hours of patient-facing work; Master's thesis in cooperation with a healthcare facility; preparation for the German nursing knowledge exam; graduate outcomes include governmental recognition of nursing qualifications in Germany and entry roles such as Advanced Practice Nurse, Case Manager, Nursing Specialist; university founded 1994 as a Bavarian state university; services including Welcome Center support, accommodation-finding assistance, Orientation Week, and free public transport in the city of Hof; named programme head Prof. Dr. Matthias Drossel; application windows (WiSe: 15 April-31 May; SuSe: 5-30 Nov) via the Primuss portal.

sources.label: Hof University - Cross Cultural Nursing Practice M.Sc. official module plan / ECTS breakdown (Studienverlaufsplan, Faculty INWISS, PDF)
sources.notes: Official Faculty of Interdisciplinary and Innovative Sciences (INWISS) module/ECTS table confirming a total of 120 ECTS across 4 semesters: German B1 and subject-specific German B2 language modules, Intercultural Trainings, Culturally Sensitive Care, Sector-Specific Assessments/Diagnostics/Interventions, Healing Competences Basics/I/II, Evidence Based Nursing Research Workshop, Clinical Leadership (semesters 1-2); Internship 1 and Internship 2 (semester 3, 18 and 12 ECTS respectively - Internship 2 continuing into semester 4 per the table's period column); and a Master's thesis with reference to professional practice, typical processing time about 5 months, worth 30 ECTS (semester 4).

sources.label: Hof University - official news article: 'Hochschule Hof will auslandische Pflegekrafte anwerben, deren Kompetenzen starken und sie integrieren' (programme launch announcement)
sources.notes: Official Hof University news article (in German) announcing the 2024 launch of the 'Cross Cultural Nursing Practice M.A.' programme, described as the only one of its kind in Germany at the time. University President Prof. Dr. Dr. h.c. Jurgen Lehmann is quoted confirming Hof University already has more than 800 Indian students successfully integrated (about two-thirds remaining in Germany as workers afterward), an office in Bangalore, and ties to the Bavarian-Indian Centre for Business and Higher Education; explicitly frames India, including in cooperation with the Catholic youth organisation Don Bosco, as a priority recruitment region alongside a stated commitment to individualised admission review. Dean Prof. Dr. Gerald Schmola confirms the German A2-to-B2 language pathway and that Germany requires German-language learning unlike English-speaking destination countries. Programme director Prof. Dr. Matthias Drossel confirms the 900-hours-minimum patient-facing internship requirement and preparation for the statutory nursing knowledge exam under Section 45 PflAPrV. Independently, a Bavarian Ministry of Health representative (Dr. Gregor Jaburek) is quoted confirming state-level efforts to centralise and speed up foreign nursing-qualification recognition procedures via Bavaria's Landesamt fur Pflege from 1 July 2023.

sources.label: Campuls (Hof University's own news portal) - English-language republication of the programme launch announcement
sources.notes: English-language version of the same official Hof University announcement, used to corroborate the German-language source above and confirm quotes in English: '800 Indian students have already been integrated with us without any problems, of which about two-thirds remain in Germany as workers', the described Skill and Grade Mix philosophy of Prof. Dr. Matthias Drossel, and confirmation that the programme targets nursing staff worldwide already pre-qualified with a bachelor's degree, with a particular institutional focus on recruitment from India.

sources.label: Bibliomed Pflege (independent German nursing-sector trade press) - 'Erster Studiengang gegen den Pflegenotstand in Hof'
sources.notes: Independent nursing-sector trade publication (dated 6 April 2023), unaffiliated with Hof University, corroborating the programme's 2024 launch, its description (by the university) as the only programme of its kind in Germany, its tuition-free structure, its focus on already-bachelor's-qualified nursing staff worldwide with a particular institutional focus on India, the German A2 entry-level language requirement, and the 900-hours minimum patient-facing internship requirement - independent confirmation that this is genuine reported news, not solely university self-promotion.

sources.label: Wikipedia - Hof University
sources.notes: Independent general-reference corroboration: Hof University of Applied Sciences (Hochschule fur Angewandte Wissenschaften Hof) was established in 1994, located in Hof, Bavaria, Germany, with roughly 3,700 students (2018 figure), about 70 academic staff and 150 administrative staff; used only for founding-year and general institutional corroboration, not for programme-specific claims.

sources.label: Wikipedia - Hof, Bavaria
sources.notes: Independent general-reference corroboration for city-context facts: Hof (also called Hof/Saale) is a town on the Saale river in the Upper Franconia region of Bavaria, close to the Czech border, in the Fichtel Mountains/Franconian Forest upland area, with a 2024 population of approximately 46,484; historically a centre of textile and porcelain manufacturing from the 19th century; used only for neutral city-context framing, not for university-specific claims.

structuredFacts.name: Hof University of Applied Sciences (Hochschule Hof)
bestFitFor: Nursing graduates who already hold a completed Bachelor's degree in Nursing (minimum 180 ECTS) and want an academic Master's pathway into the German healthcare system, rather than a first-time undergraduate nursing qualification

Students prepared to build German to at least B2 level over the course of the programme (entering at A2, per the university's own 2023-2025 published requirements) - this is not an English-only pathway, and day-to-day study, internships and clinical work from semester 2 onward happen substantially in German

Students specifically aiming for governmental recognition of their nursing qualification within Germany and a route toward advanced/specialist nursing roles (e.g. Advanced Practice Nurse, Case Manager, Clinical Leadership) rather than only entry-level ward nursing

Applicants comfortable verifying the programme's current name, structure and requirements directly with Hof University before applying, since the programme was under revision at time of writing and is expected to relaunch as 'Advanced Nursing Practice (M.Sc.)' for winter semester 2026/27
recognitionBadges: Public Bavarian state university (Hof University of Applied Sciences, founded 1994), funded and operated by the Free State of Bavaria
Programme graduates receive governmental recognition of their nursing qualification in Germany, per the university's official programme flyer
Graduates are explicitly prepared for Germany's statutory nursing knowledge examination (Kenntnisprüfung, per Section 45 PflAPrV), per the official 2024 launch announcement

recognitionLinks.label: Hof University - Cross Cultural Nursing Practice M.Sc. official flyer (career outcomes and recognition)

teachingHospitals: Two supervised clinical internships (Internship 1 and Internship 2, semester 3 per the official module plan) at a hospital, long-term inpatient facility, or home-care institution, together requiring a minimum of 900 hours of direct patient-facing work, per the official programme flyer

The Master's thesis (semester 4) is completed in cooperation with a hospital, long-term inpatient, or home-care institution, giving a research/practice link to a real clinical partner rather than a purely theoretical thesis

Specific partner hospital/clinic names were not found in the officially published materials reviewed; students should ask Hof University's programme office directly for its current list of clinical placement partners before applying

programs.title: Cross Cultural Nursing Practice (M.Sc.) - postgraduate nursing programme (expected to relaunch as 'Advanced Nursing Practice, M.Sc.' for winter semester 2026/27)
programs.medium: Mixed: semester 1 taught in English; semester 2 onward (including German B2 language modules, core nursing modules, both clinical internships, and most of the Master's thesis work) taught/conducted in German. The Master's thesis itself may be written in German or English. Entry requires English at TOEFL 90+/IELTS 6.5+ plus German at minimum A2 (CEFR), with German built up to B2 during the programme - this is not an English-medium degree.
programs.feeNotes: Confirmed tuition-free by Hof University's official programme flyer: no tuition fee is charged, only an administrative/semester fee of approximately EUR 150 per semester. No official annual or total cost-of-study figure in INR/USD is published by the university, so no tuition amount is stated here; students should budget separately for living costs, German statutory health insurance, and visa financial-resources requirements, and confirm the current administrative fee directly with Hof University before applying. IMPORTANT: at time of writing, Hof University's own current programme information indicates this Master's programme is being revised and is expected to relaunch under the name 'Advanced Nursing Practice (M.Sc.)' for winter semester 2026/27, with updated admission requirements to be published closer to the April 2026 application window - students should verify the current programme name, fee structure and requirements directly with Hof University (admission@hof-university.de) before applying.

draftContent.summary: Hof University of Applied Sciences (Hochschule Hof) is a public Bavarian state university founded in 1994 in the town of Hof, in the Upper Franconia region of northern Bavaria, enrolling roughly 3,700 students including a substantial international cohort. Through its Department/Faculty of Interdisciplinary and Innovative Sciences, Hof University created a postgraduate nursing programme - launched in 2024 as 'Cross Cultural Nursing Practice' (M.Sc.) and described by the university as the only programme of its kind in Germany at the time - built specifically for nursing graduates from outside Germany, with an explicit institutional focus on recruiting from India. The programme is a 4-semester, tuition-free Master of Science for applicants who already hold a completed Bachelor's degree in Nursing (minimum 180 ECTS), combining German-language training, intercultural and clinical-leadership coursework, two supervised clinical internships totalling at least 900 hours of direct patient care, and a practice-linked Master's thesis, leading toward governmental recognition of the nursing qualification in Germany. At time of writing, Hof University's own current programme information indicates this Master's is under revision and is expected to relaunch under the name 'Advanced Nursing Practice (M.Sc.)' for winter semester 2026/27, with finalised admission requirements to be published closer to the April 2026 application window; this profile is built from the most recent official flyer and module plan for the programme in its 'Cross Cultural Nursing Practice' form, corroborated by the university's own 2024 launch announcement, independent German nursing trade press, and general-reference sources, and prospective applicants should confirm the current programme name and requirements directly with Hof University before applying.
draftContent.campusLifestyle: Hof University's campus is centred on Alfons-Goppel-Platz in the town of Hof, with a Welcome Center, International Office and Career Service that the university's own materials describe as providing extensive personal support to international students, alongside 24-hour access to computer labs and the library and free campus wifi. The university describes an intercultural campus with German and international students studying together from more than 60 countries, plus student initiatives such as an eSports group, a Fairtrade group, and a Formula Student engineering team. For nursing-programme students specifically, the university highlights free public transportation within the city of Hof, an Orientation Week held shortly before each semester starts, and organised extracurricular activities such as intercultural trainings, field trips and company visits aimed at building both practical skills and social integration. Because meaningful parts of the programme (from semester 2 onward) are conducted in German, day-to-day campus and classroom life increasingly involves German-language interaction as the programme progresses, so students should treat the university's German-language build-up (A2 to B2) as a core part of settling into campus life, not an optional extra.
draftContent.cityProfile: Hof (also called Hof/Saale, or Hof an der Saale) is a town on the Saale river in the Upper Franconia region of northeastern Bavaria, close to the Czech border, set among the Fichtel Mountains and Franconian Forest upland areas, with a 2024 population of roughly 46,484. Historically, Hof grew into a significant centre of textile manufacturing from the 19th century (aided by an established local wool trade and, from the 15th century, cotton processing), with a porcelain industry also developing in its surrounding districts; the town's centuries-old core dates its old town settlement to around 1080, with its 'New Town' founded around 1230 and a written town charter since 1319. As a mid-sized Bavarian university town rather than a major metropolitan hub, Hof offers a comparatively calm, lower-cost setting than cities like Munich or Nuremberg, with green surroundings suited to outdoor activity, while still keeping major German cities such as Berlin and Munich within reasonable travelling distance by rail. Students relocating from India should expect a genuinely provincial German town rather than a large international city, and should independently verify current transport links, cost of living, and community/cultural resources (including any Indian student groups) directly with Hof University's International Office before arrival.
draftContent.clinicalExposure: The programme's clinical component centres on two supervised internships (Internship 1 and Internship 2, positioned in semester 3 per the official module plan, with Internship 2 continuing into semester 4), together requiring a minimum of 900 hours of direct, patient-facing work, undertaken at a hospital, long-term inpatient facility, or home-care institution. Academically, this is paired with a structured sequence of clinical-competency modules across semesters 1-2 - Culturally Sensitive Care, Sector-Specific Assessments/Diagnostics/Interventions, Healing Competences Basics/I/II, an Evidence Based Nursing Research Workshop, and Clinical Leadership - designed, per the university, to build advanced clinical decision-making and leadership skills on top of an existing nursing bachelor's qualification rather than to teach nursing fundamentals from scratch. The Master's thesis itself (semester 4, worth 30 ECTS, typically taking about 5 months per the official module plan) is completed in cooperation with a real hospital, long-term inpatient, or home-care institution, giving graduates a practice-linked research outcome. Hof University's officially published materials reviewed here do not name specific partner hospitals or clinics for these placements, so applicants should ask the programme office directly (contact: Prof. Dr. Matthias Drossel, matthias.drossel@hof-university.de, per the official flyer) for its current list of clinical placement partners and how placements are assigned before applying.
draftContent.hostelOverview: Hof University's own materials describe 'assistance in finding accommodation' as a support service provided to international students through its Welcome Center and International Office, but no official on-campus student-hostel fee schedule for this programme was found in the sources reviewed. This suggests housing is arranged through the general German/Bavarian student-accommodation system (typically a regional Studierendenwerk or the private rental market) rather than a dedicated hall of residence built specifically for this programme. Given the programme's tuition-free structure keeps overall costs comparatively low, students should budget separately and realistically for accommodation, and should contact Hof University's Welcome Center (welcome@hof-university.de) as early as possible after receiving an admission offer to confirm current accommodation options, likely costs in the town of Hof, and any required deposits, since a mid-sized university town like Hof may have a more limited private rental market than a large city.
draftContent.indianFoodSupport: Hof University's own 2024 programme launch announcement states explicitly that more than 800 Indian students had, at that point, already been successfully integrated at the university, with about two-thirds remaining in Germany as workers afterward - indicating an established and ongoing Indian student presence at Hof University, even though the officially published programme materials reviewed here do not describe a dedicated on-campus Indian mess, dining hall, or specific Indian grocery/restaurant list for the town of Hof. Given this established community, students should expect at least some existing Indian-student peer network and informal knowledge-sharing about food sourcing, though as a smaller Bavarian university town, Hof is unlikely to have the scale of South Asian grocery and restaurant options found in Germany's larger cities. Students who want reliable access to Indian, vegetarian or specific dietary options should contact Hof University's International Office or connect with current Indian students at the university (via the university's own international-student community channels) to confirm current grocery sources, restaurants, and self-catering arrangements in Hof before arrival, since most student accommodation in Germany is self-catered.
draftContent.safetyOverview: Hof is a smaller Bavarian town rather than a major city, and the university's own promotional materials describe it as 'a safe and cozy town in the heart of Europe'; while this reflects the university's own characterisation rather than independent crime statistics, Germany overall maintains standard Western European policing, healthcare, and public-infrastructure standards. International students at Hof University are required to arrange German statutory health insurance and complete standard visa and residence-registration procedures on arrival, and the university's Welcome Center and International Office are the official channels for accommodation, orientation and settling-in support - the university explicitly recommends using these official channels rather than informal agents. As with any move abroad, students should keep visa, insurance and residence-registration documentation current, confirm current safety and neighbourhood information for their specific accommodation directly with the university's International Office, and check their home government's latest travel guidance for Germany before departure.
draftContent.studentSupport: Applications for the programme (in its 'Cross Cultural Nursing Practice' form) run through Hof University's online Primuss portal, with winter-semester applications open 15 April-31 May and summer-semester applications open 5-30 November, per the official flyer; admission letters are issued in June (winter) or December (summer), with online enrolment in September (winter) or March (summer), and an international-student Orientation Week held in the final two weeks of September (winter) or first two weeks of March (summer). Named official contacts include the programme's Head of Program, Prof. Dr. Matthias Drossel (matthias.drossel@hof-university.de), and the university's Welcome Center (Carolin Huttner, welcome@hof-university.de, +49 9281 409-3319), which the university states provides organisational support before and during studies, accommodation-finding assistance, and career-promoting activities such as intercultural trainings, field trips and company visits. IMPORTANT: because this programme was under revision at time of writing and is expected to relaunch as 'Advanced Nursing Practice (M.Sc.)' for winter semester 2026/27, prospective applicants should contact Hof University's admissions team (admission@hof-university.de) directly to confirm the current programme name, updated admission requirements, and current application windows before beginning an application, rather than relying solely on the 2023-2025 'Cross Cultural Nursing Practice' materials referenced in this profile.
whyChoose: A public Bavarian state university (est. 1994) offering a genuinely tuition-free Master's pathway for already-qualified nursing graduates - only a modest administrative fee of approximately EUR 150/semester, per the official programme flyer, rather than the per-semester tuition typical of private institutions.

An institutional track record and stated focus on recruiting and integrating nursing graduates from India specifically: Hof University's own 2024 launch announcement states more than 800 Indian students have already been integrated at the university, with about two-thirds remaining in Germany as workers, plus an existing university office in Bangalore and ties to the Bavarian-Indian Centre for Business and Higher Education.

A structured route toward governmental recognition of a nursing qualification within Germany, combined with substantial hands-on clinical experience (a minimum of 900 hours across two supervised internships) rather than a purely classroom-based postgraduate degree.

A clear, staged German-language pathway (A2 at entry, built up to B2 during the programme) integrated directly into the curriculum, rather than requiring students to independently reach an advanced German level before applying.
thingsToConsider: This is not an English-medium programme: only semester 1 is taught in English, per the official flyer; from semester 2 onward, German-language modules, core nursing coursework, both clinical internships, and most Master's thesis work are conducted in German (the thesis itself may be written in German or English). Entry requires German only at A2 level, so a real, sustained language-learning effort during the programme is required to reach the B2 level needed for advanced coursework and clinical placements.

The programme was under revision at time of writing and is expected to relaunch under a new name, 'Advanced Nursing Practice (M.Sc.)', for winter semester 2026/27, with Hof University stating that finalised admission requirements will be published closer to the April 2026 application window - applicants should treat the 'Cross Cultural Nursing Practice' details in this profile as the most recent published basis for the programme, not as guaranteed final requirements, and should confirm current details directly with Hof University before applying.

This programme is designed for nursing graduates who already hold a completed Bachelor's degree in Nursing (minimum 180 ECTS) - it is not a first-time nursing qualification, so students without an existing nursing bachelor's degree are not eligible.

Recognition of this German postgraduate nursing qualification for future practice in India is not asserted here: the Indian Nursing Council (INC) evaluates foreign nursing qualifications through its own case-by-case equivalency process, so students who plan to eventually practise in India after this Master's should contact the INC directly and confirm current equivalency requirements before enrolling.

Hof is a smaller Bavarian university town rather than a large city, and no official on-campus student-hostel fee schedule or dedicated Indian dining facility was found in the materials reviewed - students should independently confirm current accommodation costs, availability, and food/grocery options in Hof directly with the university's Welcome Center before committing.

faq.question: Is Hof University's nursing Master's programme taught in English?
faq.answer: Only partly. Per Hof University's official programme flyer, semester 1 is taught in English, but from semester 2 onward - German-language modules, core nursing coursework, both clinical internships, and most Master's thesis work - are conducted in German. Entry requires German only at A2 level (CEFR), with the programme designed to build this up to B2 over the course of study. Applicants should not expect a fully English-medium degree.

faq.question: What are the admission requirements for this programme?
faq.answer: Per Hof University's official flyer for the programme as most recently published ('Cross Cultural Nursing Practice', 2025 edition), applicants need a completed Bachelor's degree in Nursing (minimum 180 ECTS), English proficiency (TOEFL 90+ or IELTS 6.5 or above), and German at a minimum of A2 level (CEFR), proven via official test documents such as Goethe, telc or OSD. Because the programme is expected to relaunch under the name 'Advanced Nursing Practice (M.Sc.)' for winter semester 2026/27, prospective applicants should confirm final, updated requirements directly with Hof University closer to the April 2026 application window.

faq.question: How much does this Master's programme cost?
faq.answer: It is tuition-free: Hof University's official flyer confirms no tuition fee is charged, only an administrative/semester fee of approximately EUR 150. Living costs, health insurance and visa financial-resources requirements are separate and should be budgeted independently; students should confirm the current administrative fee and any other costs directly with Hof University before applying.

faq.question: What clinical training does the programme include?
faq.answer: The programme includes two supervised clinical internships (in semester 3, continuing into semester 4 per the official module plan) at a hospital, long-term inpatient facility, or home-care institution, together requiring a minimum of 900 hours of direct patient-facing work, per the official programme flyer. This is paired with clinical-competency coursework (Culturally Sensitive Care, Sector-Specific Assessments/Diagnostics/Interventions, Healing Competences modules, Clinical Leadership) and a Master's thesis completed in cooperation with a healthcare facility.

faq.question: Is this programme currently open for applications?
faq.answer: At time of writing, Hof University's own current programme information indicates this Master's is being revised and is expected to relaunch under the name 'Advanced Nursing Practice (M.Sc.)' for winter semester 2026/27, with finalised admission requirements to be published closer to the April 2026 application window. Prospective applicants should contact Hof University's admissions team (admission@hof-university.de) directly to confirm the current programme name, status and application timeline before applying.

faq.question: Will this qualification let me practise nursing in India afterwards?
faq.answer: This is not guaranteed and is not asserted here. The Indian Nursing Council (INC) assesses foreign nursing qualifications, including German ones, through its own case-by-case equivalency process, rather than issuing blanket recognition to any single foreign programme. Students who plan to practise in India after this Master's should contact the INC directly and confirm the current equivalency process and requirements before enrolling.

faq.question: Does Hof University have experience with Indian students specifically?
faq.answer: Yes. Hof University's own 2024 launch announcement for this programme states that more than 800 Indian students had, at that point, already been successfully integrated at the university, with about two-thirds remaining in Germany as workers afterward, and that the university maintains an office in Bangalore and ties to the Bavarian-Indian Centre for Business and Higher Education. The programme was designed with a stated institutional focus on recruiting nursing graduates from India.
